content.question: If Hungary were to abolish age-10/12 tracks but retain school-choice freedom and permit within-school class stratification (as Finland did from the 1990s onward), what institutional safeguards—funding equalization, curricular parity, teacher placement rules—would be necessary to prevent segregation from re-emerging through choice and internal class mechanisms?
content.reduction_path: Conduct a prospective policy-simulation study using agent-based modeling or comparative case studies of post-comprehensive systems (Flanders, German Länder, Nordic municipalities) that differ in choice regulation and within-school grouping rules; identify the minimal institutional bundle that maintained segregation control
